differential_diagnoses: Viperid envenomation (Echis ocellatus) complicated by systemic coagulopathy and venom-induced consumption coagulopathy
Placental abruption secondary to venom-induced coagulopathy
Acute kidney injury (likely acute tubular necrosis or acute cortical necrosis)
Compartment syndrome of the lower leg
next_steps: Immediate removal of the tourniquet under controlled conditions
Administration of appropriate species-specific polyvalent antivenom
Urgent blood typing, cross-matching, and transfusion of packed red blood cells and fresh frozen plasma/cryoprecipitate
Continuous maternal hemodynamic and fetal heart rate monitoring
Placement of an indwelling urinary catheter for strict input/output measurement
Prepare for emergency cesarean section due to life-threatening placental abruption and maternal coagulopathy
Urgent transfer to a tertiary center equipped for hemodialysis and advanced obstetric care
